objective: build context usefulness eval for .agents using outcome-level A/B, harmful-context sentinels, and MemRL feedback integration

tracker_health.reason: Created epic soc-rn8k and six implementation tasks. Hierarchical child creation hit a prefix mismatch after soc-rn8k.7, so the remaining tasks are recorded in the epic notes as standalone task beads.

done_criteria: context usefulness contract exists and is catalogued
deterministic context A/B eval runner supports context_off, context_on, curated_only, stale_sentinel, and toxic_sentinel variants
fixtures prove helpful context, ignored stale context, and penalized harmful context without writing sentinels into the live .agents corpus
scorecard records task success delta, token/tool delta, source contradiction, harmful uptake, known-failure avoidance, and per-artifact attribution
MemRL and maturity use scorecard attribution rather than citation alone
corpus fitness, Dream, and gates expose context usefulness or a clear degraded reason
docs explain hygiene versus retrieval versus outcome usefulness

test_levels.rationale: The slice adds CLI eval behavior, fixture isolation, lifecycle feedback, corpus fitness, Dream/gate surfaces, and product docs. Deterministic SIL and focused integration tests are required; broader runtime variance work remains advisory.
